problem text: Assume $X$ and $Y$ are standard normal random variables that are independent. Determine $\mathbb{P}[Y > 4X]$.
problem solution: Since $X$ and $Y$ are normal random variables with mean $0$, the random variable $Y - 4X$ is also normal with a mean of $0$. Thus, $\mathbb{P}[Y > 4X] = \mathbb{P}[Y - 4X > 0]$ represents the probability that a normal random variable exceeds its mean. By the symmetry of the normal distribution around its mean, this probability is $\dfrac{1}{2}$.
problem hint: What is the probability distribution of $Y - 4X$?
